Bacterial_spot.summary: Bacterial spot: small, dark, water-soaked lesions on leaves, stems, or fruit. Caused by Xanthomonas. Favors warm, wet conditions.
Bacterial_spot.treatment: Remove affected plant parts. Apply copper-based bactericide. Avoid overhead watering. Use disease-free seed; rotate crops.

Early_blight.summary: Early blight: brown concentric rings on older leaves, stem cankers. Caused by Alternaria solani.
Early_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides (e.g. chlorothalonil). Improve air flow; mulch; avoid wet foliage. Remove infected leaves.

Late_blight.summary: Late blight: irregular greasy patches, white mold in humidity. Caused by Phytophthora infestans.
Late_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides (e.g. copper, specific late-blight products). Destroy severely infected plants; avoid overhead irrigation.

Leaf_Mold.summary: Leaf mold: yellow patches on upper leaf, fuzzy olive-gray mold underneath. Fungal, thrives in humidity.
Leaf_Mold.treatment: Reduce humidity and improve ventilation. Apply fungicide if needed. Remove badly infected leaves.

Septoria_leaf_spot.summary: Septoria leaf spot: small circular spots with gray centers and dark margins. Fungal.
Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply fungicide (e.g. chlorothalonil). Avoid overhead watering; rotate crops.

Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider_mite.summary: Two-spotted spider mite: stippling, webbing, leaves can yellow and drop. Mites thrive in hot, dry conditions.
Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider_mite.treatment: Increase humidity; spray leaves with water. Use miticide or horticultural oil if needed. Remove heavily infested leaves.

Target_Spot.summary: Target spot: dark concentric rings on leaves and fruit. Fungal (Corynespora).
Target_Spot.treatment: Apply fungicide. Improve air flow; avoid wet foliage. Remove and destroy severely affected material.

Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.summary: Tomato yellow leaf curl virus: curled, yellowed leaves, stunted growth. Spread by whiteflies.
Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: No cure. Remove infected plants. Control whiteflies; use resistant varieties and virus-free transplants.

Tomato_mosaic_virus.summary: Tomato mosaic virus: mottled, distorted leaves; reduced yield. Spread by contact and pests.
Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: No cure. Remove infected plants. Disinfect tools; control aphids; use resistant varieties and clean seed.

healthy.summary: No significant disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
healthy.treatment: Continue good practices: even watering, airflow, and monitoring for pests or disease.

powdery_mildew.summary: Powdery mildew: white powdery patches on leaves. Fungal; common in warm, humid or dry conditions.
powdery_mildew.treatment: Improve air flow; avoid excess nitrogen. Apply sulfur or other fungicide labeled for powdery mildew.
